program.name: Transurban graduate program: Journey into the Future
program.close_date_raw: Applications for our 2025 graduate program have closed.
program.duration_text: 18-month rotational program (three six-month rotations in a core discipline).
program.rotation_text: Three six-month rotations in a core discipline.

eligibility.graduation_window: Intent to complete tertiary studies by February 2025 (or have completed in the last two years).
disciplines: Science (Engineering Science, Computer and Software Systems)
Technology (Information Technology, Business Information Systems, Computer Systems, Computer Science, Software Engineering or Software Development)
minimum_requirements: You intend to complete tertiary studies by February 2025 (or you have completed in the last two years).
You have (or will complete) a relevant bachelor’s degree with a credit average in one of the listed disciplines.

stages.name: Step 1: Apply online
stages.details: Apply online. Applications are currently closed.

stages.name: Step 2: Psychometric testing
stages.details: Complete online assessments that examine your abilities and preferences when working with others, across changing conditions, and with different types of information. Instructions and practice questions are provided in advance.

stages.name: Step 3: Video interview
stages.details: Complete a one-way video assessment where you record answers to set questions. This is used to assess your strengths, motivations, and alignment to Transurban’s core values.

stages.name: Steps 4 and 5: Presentation and interview
stages.details: Attend interactive, practical activities that may include a presentation and an interview. The presentation looks at how you work with others to solve a business problem and how you reflect. The interview includes structured behavioural questions aligned to the role requirements, and you can ask questions about the program.

stages.name: Step 6: Offer and reference checks
stages.details: If progressed, Transurban contacts your nominated referees for reference checks.

stages.name: Step 7: Onboarding commences
stages.details: You are introduced to the prior graduate cohort, your people leader, and your buddy before your formal start date, and you learn more about how Transurban operates.

stages.name: Step 8: First day
stages.details: You are welcomed by your people leader and team, complete a facilities induction, and later in the first month you complete a corporate induction. Your official graduate experience then commences.

tips: Follow Transurban on LinkedIn to stay up to date with Graduate and Student Community news.
Use the video interview to clearly explain your strengths, motivations, and how you align to Transurban’s core values.
Prepare to demonstrate how you work with others to solve practical business problems during the presentation and interview stage.

exec_themes: Grow traffic and toll revenue by operating high-quality toll road assets and improving the customer travel experience.
Expand capacity through a long-term project pipeline (major new projects plus smaller, repeatable network enhancements).
Improve performance through active cost management and operating efficiency.
Prioritise North America as a growth engine, highlighting a “step change” in operational performance after completing key projects.
Work with government on NSW toll reform to create a simpler and more transparent system while protecting contract value and revenue.
Invest in technology and innovation to help customers make better travel choices and to improve end-to-end journeys.
Manage the balance sheet to fund growth while navigating a higher interest-rate (“macroeconomic”) environment.
commercial_context.profit_engine: Transurban’s core profit engine is collecting toll revenue from long-life toll road concessions. Cash generation is supported by traffic volumes (average daily trips), tolling outcomes, and disciplined control of operating costs, which flows through to EBITDA and Free Cash used to pay distributions.
commercial_context.headwinds: Key headwinds explicitly flagged include: (1) macroeconomic factors that can affect traffic and distribution outcomes; (2) inflation pressure on costs (even if partly offset by cost management); (3) interest costs and refinancing timing (early refinancing brought forward interest costs); and (4) regulatory/policy uncertainty and negotiation complexity around NSW toll reform (changes are being worked through with government, with outcomes to be finalised in 1H 2026).
commercial_context.esg: Transurban states it is committed to net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 (Scopes 1, 2, and 3), with interim science-based targets including a 50% reduction in absolute Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2030. It also describes actions such as improving road efficiency, shifting its own operations away from fossil fuels, and reducing supply-chain emissions intensity. In its investor materials it also reports sourcing high levels of renewable energy across the group and progress reducing Scope 1 and 2 emissions year-on-year.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Transurban describes a “next phase of growth” after completing several key strategic initiatives and projects, including major capacity coming online (notably in North America and Melbourne). It also signals a more customer-focused approach through reforms (e.g., removing certain administration fees by mid-2026 as part of NSW toll reform discussions) and continued investment in innovation/technology to improve the end-to-end travel experience.

notes: The program name, locations, streams, and rotation/duration details are stated on the Graduate Program page.
The page states that applications for the 2025 graduate program have closed, but it does not provide a specific close date.
Eligibility details are taken directly from the 'About you' section on the Graduate Program page.
Work rights requirements are not explicitly stated on the page.
Recruitment steps are listed in order on the Graduate Program page under 'Next steps and process'.
No application open/close dates are provided beyond 'Applications are currently closed'.
Some fields (especially profit_engine and recent_pivot) require synthesis across multiple official documents; Transurban does not present them as a single labeled section.

The 1H26 ASX release provides clear statements on strategic priorities, macro/interest-rate context, and project completion signals; however it is a short document, so some themes may be under-specified versus the full corporate report.

Net-zero and emissions targets are clearly stated on Transurban sustainability pages; broader ESG topics (e.g., safety, community investment, governance) were not extracted here because they were not as explicit in the specific sources reviewed in this run.
